name: Things 3 MCP Server
what_it_does: Things 3 MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Things 3 — the popular macOS/iOS task manager — creating tasks, managing to-dos, reading task lists, and integrating Things 3's GTD-style task management into agent-driven productivity workflows on Apple platforms.
use_cases: Creating and updating tasks in Things 3 from productivity agents
Reading today's task list from daily planning agents
Adding tasks to inbox from capture agents processing notes or emails
Managing project tasks from project management agents
Integrating Things 3 with calendar and email agents for unified workflow
Querying and completing tasks from focus session agents
not_for: Teams needing shared task management (Things 3 is personal)
Todoist, Linear, or JIRA users — use appropriate MCPs
best_when: A macOS user with Things 3 needs AI agents to read from and write to their personal task manager as part of productivity automation.
avoid_when: You don't use Things 3, or need team/shared task management — use Todoist, Linear, or JIRA MCPs.

auth.notes: No authentication — communicates with Things 3 via URL schemes or AppleScript on macOS. Things 3 must be installed and running.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Things 3 requires paid purchase ($49.99 macOS, $9.99 iOS)
pricing.paid_tiers: Things 3: one-time purchase; MCP server is free open source
pricing.notes: Community MCP is free. Things 3 app requires one-time purchase from App Store.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no rate limits; Things 3 URL scheme limits may apply

known_agent_gotchas: Things 3 must be running on macOS for URL scheme-based operations
URL scheme API has limited write operations compared to read
Things 3 does not have an official read API — reading tasks may use AppleScript or unofficial methods
Apple Silicon/Intel differences may affect AppleScript behavior
Community MCP — Things 3 URL scheme changes can break functionality
Task creation via URL scheme has limited field support vs full Things API
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local macOS integration. No credentials. No network access. Community MCP. Personal task data stays local. AppleScript may require accessibility permissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local macOS. No network. No credentials. AppleScript may need accessibility permissions.
